item.title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60 billion
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Only Elon would do this before an IPO.

item.title: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple: Here’s a look at his 15-year legacy, from new products and services to China expansion
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Cook, who joined Apple in 1998, succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO in 2011 and went on to transform Apple into a $4 trillion powerhouse.

item.title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

item.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Founded by an OSU researcher, the startup is developing AI agents that can become experts in any domain.

item.title: Apple’s Cal AI crackdown signals it’s still policing the App Store
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple tells TechCrunch Cal AI was removed not for web payments alone, but for deceptive billing, manipulative tactics, and App Store rule violations.

item.title: SusHi Tech Tokyo isn’t a conference — it’s a deal room with 60,000 people
item.dc:creator: Cindy Zackney
item.description: When 60,000 attendees descend on Tokyo Big Sight April 27–29, the headline numbers are hard to ignore: 750 startup exhibitors, 151 sessions, city leaders from 49 countries. But the stat that tells you what kind of event this actually is? It's 10,000 facilitated business meetings — brokered, booked, and tracked before most attendees even land.

item.title: ChatGPT’s new Images 2.0 model is surprisingly good at generating text
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0, the newest image-generation model from OpenAI, shows just how much AI capabilities have evolved over the last few years.

item.title: Sam Altman throws shade at Anthropic’s cyber model, Mythos: ‘fear-based marketing’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: This week, during a podcast appearance, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called out his competitor's new cybersecurity model, noting that the company was using fear to make its product sound more impressive than it actually is.

item.title: Clarifai deletes 3 million photos that OkCupid provided to train facial recognition AI, report says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The photo deletion comes after an FTC settlement with Clarifai. The company had asked OkCupid — whose executives had invested in Clarifai — to share data in 2014, according to court documents.

item.title: Former Pinterest team redesigns email with Extra — and it’s actually good
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Extra, from a team of former Pinterest designers and engineers, reimagines email around your life instead of the traditional inbox.

item.title: AI Dungeon maker Latitude unveils Voyage, a platform for creating AI-powered RPGs
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Latitude's new AI-native platform, Voyage, aims to help gamers create their very own role-playing game.

item.title: Ransomware negotiator pleads guilty to helping ransomware gang
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: A former employee of a cybersecurity firm pleaded guilty to aiding ransomware criminals to maximize their profits, with the goal of taking a cut of the ransom.

item.title: Bond, a new social media platform, wants to use AI to help you kick your doomscrolling habit
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Bond wants you to get off the couch and get back into the real world, its creator says. The new platform's AI system is designed to motivate users to do things away from the app.

item.title: YouTube expands its AI likeness detection technology to celebrities
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: YouTube is expanding its AI likeness detection tool to celebrities, giving talent and their reps a way to find and remove deepfakes.

item.title: Amazon taps Sweden’s Einride for its electric big rigs
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The agreement comes just as Einride is finalizing a merger with blank-check company Legato Merger Corp. to go public.

item.title: Revolut eyes valuation of up to $200B in eventual IPO
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: Fintech giant Revolut, which secured a full banking license in the U.K. in March after years of waiting, was most recently valued at $75 billion in a secondary share sale.

item.title: With US spy laws set to expire, lawmakers are split over protecting Americans from warrantless surveillance
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Some lawmakers are calling for widespread reforms following years of surveillance scandals and abuses across successive U.S. administrations. But even if the spy law known as Section 702 expires on April 30, the government's spy powers will not automatically lapse.

item.title: What’s the key to better vegan cheese? Microbreweries, one startup says.
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: AuX Labs says it has found a better way to make vegan cheese that tastes and feels like the real thing — and costs the same.

item.title: Cash App is targeting a new kind of customer: 6- to 12-year-olds
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The company is expanding its youth-focused services in an effort to build a relationship with Gen Alpha and the upcoming generation of adolescents in the U.S.

item.title: GRAI believes AI can make music more social, not replace artists
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: AI music startup GRAI says fans want to remix tracks, not generate songs from scratch.
